title: [Bug]: README restructuring removed mission-support use-case discoverability link
summary: The README front-door restructuring for issue #343 removed the explicit repository link to the mission-support use-case index that the documentation discoverability test requires.
observed: `scripts/check.sh` fails in `tests/unit/test_mission_support_examples.py::test_mission_support_scenarios_are_discoverable` because `README.md` no longer contains `docs/use-cases/mission-support/index.md`.
expected: The README should remain concise while still linking to the mission-support operational examples in a way that keeps the public documentation entry point discoverable.
reproduction: Run `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_mission_support_examples.py::test_mission_support_scenarios_are_discoverable -q` after the README restructuring.
failing_test: The existing focused discoverability test already proves the regression and fails before the fix.
impact: This is a low-severity documentation discoverability regression. It does not affect runtime delivery, ACK ordering, sink writes, idempotency, or package execution.
delivery_semantics: No delivery semantics are affected. The fix must not change NATS, JetStream, ACK, DLQ, retry, idempotency, Oracle, or file sink behavior.
security: The fix must not add sensitive examples, private paths, live endpoints, credentials, classified data, or payload content to README or documentation.
acceptance: README contains a concise mission-support examples link that includes `docs/use-cases/mission-support/index.md`.
The focused mission-support discoverability test passes.
The README remains concise and still links detailed capability and status content to dedicated docs pages.
tests: Run `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_mission_support_examples.py::test_mission_support_scenarios_are_discoverable -q`, `scripts/check-docs.sh`, and `scripts/check.sh` after the fix.
documentation: Update README only if the missing link can be restored concisely. No runtime docs are required beyond preserving the discoverability link.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, focused regression evidence, full verification evidence, and the README link that was restored.
